description: Show all options at once for fast scanning and one-tap selection
do: Use radio group with visible labels
dont: Use dropdown that hides options behind a click
code_good: <fieldset><legend>Plan</legend><label><input type=radio name=plan value=free>Free</label><label><input type=radio name=plan value=pro>Pro</label></fieldset>

description: When options are peers and switch a view or mode use a segmented control
do: Use segmented control with clear active state
dont: Use radio buttons for view switching or tabs

description: Use native select or custom dropdown — scannable without overwhelming the form
do: Use select element with clear label
dont: Use 15 radios in a vertical stack

description: Use a filterable combobox so the user can type to narrow options
do: Use combobox with typeahead and keyboard arrows
dont: Use a plain dropdown requiring scroll through 50 items

description: Use async autocomplete with debounced search and virtualized results
do: Use async combobox with 250ms debounce and show results on type
dont: Force user to scroll a dropdown of hundreds of items

description: Use cascader or tree-select for parent-child relationships (category>subcategory)
do: Use cascader that shows path (Electronics > Phones > Android)
dont: Flatten hierarchy into one giant flat list

description: Show all checkboxes so user sees every option and can multi-tap fast
do: Use checkbox group inside a fieldset with legend
dont: Use multi-select dropdown that hides choices

description: Use multi-select dropdown that shows selected items as removable chips
do: Use searchable multi-select with chip display of selections
dont: Use 20 stacked checkboxes or select multiple (shift-click is hidden UX)

description: Use a transfer list (two panels: available / selected) so user can batch-move
do: Use transfer list with search on both panels for 20+ multi-selects
dont: Use 100 checkboxes or multi-select where selection overflows viewport

description: Use tag input where user types and presses Enter/comma to add chip
do: Use tag input with backspace to delete last chip and duplicate prevention
dont: Use comma-separated text field with no visual chips

description: Use a toggle switch when the result is immediate (settings / prefs)
do: Use toggle switch with clear on/off visual state
dont: Use a checkbox for instant-apply settings — looks like a form submit

description: Use a single checkbox when the user opts into something within a form submit
do: Use checkbox with a clear label describing the opt-in
dont: Use toggle switch that looks like it applies instantly but doesn't

description: Use slider when approximate value is fine and range is bounded (volume price filter)
do: Use slider with visible current value and min/max labels
dont: Use slider for precise values like age birth year SSN

description: Use stepper with +/- buttons for quantities 1-20 or precise small numbers
do: Use stepper with clear increment and manual input fallback
dont: Use slider for quantity in a cart (precision matters)

description: Use dual-thumb slider for min/max filters (price range date range)
do: Use dual-thumb slider with two labeled values
dont: Use two separate sliders or text inputs when values are paired

description: Use number input with formatting (commas currency) when precision matters
do: Use number input with inputmode=numeric and locale formatting
dont: Use slider for salary income or bank balance

description: Use star rating (or thumbs) for simple satisfaction capture
do: Use star rating with keyboard arrow support and hover preview
dont: Use radio group numbered 1-5 (slower to scan)

description: Use horizontal segmented scale (Strongly Disagree → Strongly Agree) with 5-7 points
do: Use segmented radio with endpoint labels visible
dont: Use dropdown asking Likert position (hides the scale)

description: Use 11-button numeric row 0-10 with color gradient from red to green
do: Use segmented row with all 11 options visible and anchor labels
dont: Use slider or dropdown for NPS (breaks benchmark comparability)

description: Use calendar date picker with text input fallback
do: Use date picker with keyboard navigation and min/max constraints
dont: Use three separate dropdowns for day/month/year

description: Use date picker that lets user pick year first (avoid 100 clicks back)
do: Use date picker with year dropdown and month dropdown in header
dont: Use calendar requiring user to click back 30 years month by month

description: Use date range picker with two-month calendar view
do: Use range picker with preset ranges (Last 7 days This month) and clear start/end
dont: Use two independent date pickers unlinked

description: Use time picker with 15-minute default steps or free input
do: Use time picker with AM/PM based on locale
dont: Use two dropdowns for hour/minute stacked

description: Use combined datetime picker or date picker with time slot grid
do: Use datetime picker with timezone if relevant
dont: Use separate date and time inputs with no validation

description: Use file input with drag-and-drop zone and visible preview
do: Use dropzone that accepts click or drag and shows thumbnail/name
dont: Use bare <input type=file> with no feedback

description: Use a dropzone that supports multiple files with per-file progress and remove
do: Use dropzone with per-file progress bar and error handling
dont: Use <input type=file multiple> with no progress indication

description: Use swatch grid of brand-approved colors for common cases full picker only when needed
do: Use swatch picker with Custom option that opens full HSL picker
dont: Show full color wheel for brand-color selection

description: Use single-line text input for names emails short answers
do: Use text input with type hints (type=email inputmode=email)
dont: Use textarea for a single-line answer

description: Use auto-resizing textarea for content that could span multiple paragraphs
do: Use textarea that grows with content up to max-height then scrolls
dont: Use fixed-height tiny textarea forcing scroll for long input
code_bad: <input type=text> for a bio or description

description: Use rich text editor only when formatting (bold lists links) is required
do: Use minimal rich text editor with just needed formatting options
dont: Use full Word-like editor when plain text or markdown works
column13: Full WYSIWYG for a comment box

description: Use search input with debounced autocomplete and recent-search suggestions
do: Use search input type=search with clear button and suggestions dropdown
dont: Use plain text input with no suggestions or feedback

description: Use password input with show/hide toggle and strength meter on creation
do: Use type=password with eye toggle and live strength feedback
dont: Use masked input with no reveal option (users can't verify typos)

description: Use specialized phone input with country selector and format mask
do: Use intl-tel-input with flag and format per country
dont: Use plain text field expecting any format

description: Use address autocomplete (Google Places) that fills structured fields
do: Use places autocomplete then reveal editable line1/city/state/zip
dont: Use one giant text field for full address

description: Use drag-to-reorder with keyboard alternative (up/down buttons + announce)
do: Use sortable list with drag handle and keyboard reorder fallback
dont: Use index number dropdowns next to each item

issue: reveal fields based on prior answer
description: Hide fields irrelevant to prior answers — show when needed
do: Use progressive disclosure: reveal follow-up fields only after trigger answer
dont: Show all 40 fields upfront with most greyed out

description: When a group has 7+ options group into labeled subsections (visual chunking)
do: Split long checkbox lists into grouped fieldsets with subheadings
dont: Present a wall of 50 ungrouped checkboxes

description: Hide rarely-used fields behind a collapsible Advanced section
do: Use collapsed Advanced panel with clear disclosure indicator
dont: Show 20 optional fields inline with required ones

description: Validate on blur or submit — never on every keystroke (premature and noisy)
do: Show error below field on blur or submit with aria-describedby
dont: Flash errors while user is still typing the first character

description: Mark required fields clearly with asterisk or (required) and aria-required
do: Use * next to label with a legend explaining * = required and aria-required=true
dont: Mark optional fields instead (users assume all required by default is wrong too)
